alabama.name: Alabama

statePrograms.name: Step Up Program
statePrograms.description: Down payment assistance up to 3% of the purchase price for qualifying buyers with incomes up to $130,600.

alabama.metaTitle: Alabama Mortgage Calculator 2026 | AL Home Loan Payment Estimator
alabama.metaDescription: Calculate your Alabama mortgage payment with current AL rates and property taxes (avg 0.41%). One of the lowest property tax rates in the nation. Free calculator for 2026.
alabama.intro: Alabama offers some of the most affordable homeownership in the Southeast, with a median home price around $220,000 and property taxes among the lowest in the nation at just 0.41%. Huntsville's aerospace and defense sector and Birmingham's healthcare economy are driving steady demand.
alabama.taxContent: Alabama's 0.41% property tax rate is one of the lowest in the U.S. — on a $220,000 home, that's roughly $75/month. The Homestead Exemption reduces assessed value for owner-occupied primary residences, and the state income tax (2–5%) is moderate.
alabama.insuranceContent: Coastal counties like Mobile and Baldwin face elevated hurricane risk, pushing annual premiums to $2,500–$4,000. Inland areas (Birmingham, Huntsville) average $1,200–$1,800/year. Budget $100–$200/month depending on location.
alabama.marketContent: Huntsville has emerged as a standout market driven by NASA, Redstone Arsenal, and a growing tech sector. Birmingham offers urban amenities at suburban prices. Much of rural Alabama qualifies for USDA zero-down loans.

alaska.name: Alaska

statePrograms.name: AHFC First Home Program
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rates for first-time homebuyers purchasing a primary residence in Alaska.

alaska.metaTitle: Alaska Mortgage Calculator 2026 | AK Home Loan Payment Estimator
alaska.metaDescription: Calculate your Alaska mortgage payment. Alaska has no state income tax and a high conforming loan limit of $1,149,825. Free AK mortgage calculator for 2026.
alaska.intro: Alaska has no state income tax and no state sales tax, and its conforming loan limit of $1,149,825 reflects the elevated home prices in Anchorage and other markets. The state's unique geography and oil-driven economy create a housing market unlike any other.
alaska.taxContent: Alaska has no state income tax and no sales tax. Property taxes vary by municipality — Anchorage averages around 1.2%. Alaska's Permanent Fund Dividend provides annual payments to residents that some buyers use toward housing costs.
alaska.insuranceContent: Alaska experiences more earthquakes than any other U.S. state, and standard homeowners insurance does not cover earthquake damage. Separate earthquake insurance averages $800–$2,000/year. Standard insurance is moderate at $900–$1,400/year.
alaska.marketContent: Alaska's housing market is concentrated in Anchorage, closely tied to oil prices and federal employment. The high conforming loan limit ($1,149,825) means most buyers can use conventional financing without a jumbo loan.

arizona.name: Arizona

statePrograms.name: Home Plus Program
statePrograms.description: Down payment assistance of 3–5% of the loan amount combined with a 30-year fixed-rate mortgage.

arizona.metaTitle: Arizona Mortgage Calculator 2026 | AZ Home Loan Payment Estimator
arizona.metaDescription: Calculate your Arizona mortgage payment with current AZ rates and property taxes (avg 0.62%). Arizona is a community property state. Market has cooled from 2022 peak. Free AZ calculator 2026.
arizona.intro: Arizona experienced one of the most dramatic housing booms and corrections in the nation between 2020 and 2024. After Phoenix surged past $450,000, the market has cooled, offering buyers more negotiating leverage. Arizona's low property tax (0.62%) and warm climate continue to attract retirees and remote workers.
arizona.taxContent: Arizona's 0.62% property tax rate is well below the national average. Residential properties are assessed at 10% of full cash value. Arizona is a community property state, and the state income tax was reduced to a flat 2.5% in 2023.
arizona.insuranceContent: Wildfire risk is significant in forested areas around Flagstaff and Prescott. Phoenix metro insurance averages $1,200–$1,600/year. Monsoon season brings flash flooding risk in low-lying areas. Budget $100–$135/month.
arizona.marketContent: Phoenix saw dramatic appreciation during 2020–2022 followed by a correction in 2023–2024. The market has stabilized with more inventory and less competition. Scottsdale and Paradise Valley remain premium markets. Tucson offers significantly more affordable options.

arkansas.name: Arkansas

statePrograms.name: ADFA Move-Up Program
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rates and down payment assistance for qualifying homebuyers in Arkansas.

arkansas.metaTitle: Arkansas Mortgage Calculator 2026 | AR Home Loan Payment Estimator
arkansas.metaDescription: Calculate your Arkansas mortgage payment. Arkansas median home price ~$195K with low property taxes (0.62%). One of the most affordable states for homebuyers. Free calculator 2026.
arkansas.intro: Arkansas consistently ranks among the most affordable states for homeownership, with a median home price around $195,000 and low property taxes. The Northwest Arkansas corridor — anchored by Walmart's headquarters in Bentonville — has emerged as one of the fastest-growing regions in the South.
arkansas.taxContent: Arkansas's 0.62% property tax rate is well below the national average. The Homestead Property Tax Credit reduces bills by up to $375/year. The state income tax top rate is 4.7%, declining as part of ongoing tax reform.
arkansas.insuranceContent: Arkansas faces tornado, severe thunderstorm, and ice storm risk. Annual insurance averages $1,400–$2,000. The Mississippi River Delta region can experience flooding. Budget $120–$170/month.
arkansas.marketContent: Northwest Arkansas (Fayetteville, Bentonville, Rogers) has seen significant growth driven by Walmart, Tyson Foods, and tech. Central Arkansas (Little Rock) offers a stable, affordable market. Much of rural Arkansas qualifies for USDA loans.

california.name: California

statePrograms.name: CalHFA MyHome Assistance Program
statePrograms.description: Deferred-payment junior loan of up to 3.5% of the purchase price for down payment and/or closing costs.

california.metaTitle: California Mortgage Calculator 2026 | CA Home Loan Payment Estimator
california.metaDescription: Calculate your California mortgage payment. CA median home price ~$750K, Prop 13 property tax caps, earthquake insurance considerations. Community property state. Free CA calculator 2026.
california.intro: California has the highest home prices of any continental U.S. state, with a statewide median around $750,000 and significantly higher in the Bay Area. Proposition 13 keeps property taxes low for existing owners, but new buyers are assessed at current market value. The state income tax (up to 13.3%) is a major affordability factor.
california.taxContent: California's Prop 13 caps annual property tax increases at 2% for existing owners, but new buyers are assessed at current market value — on a $750,000 home, expect ~$7,500–$9,750/year in property taxes. California is a community property state with the highest state income tax in the nation (up to 13.3%).
california.insuranceContent: Earthquake risk is significant statewide — separate earthquake insurance through the California Earthquake Authority averages $800–$3,000/year. Wildfire risk has caused many insurers to exit the market. Budget $1,500–$4,000+/year depending on location.
california.marketContent: The Bay Area remains one of the most expensive markets globally. Los Angeles County median is $800,000–$900,000. Sacramento and the Inland Empire offer more affordable entry points. California's $1,149,825 conforming limit helps many buyers avoid jumbo loans.

colorado.name: Colorado

statePrograms.name: CHFA FirstStep Program
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rate mortgages with optional down payment assistance for first-time homebuyers.

colorado.metaTitle: Colorado Mortgage Calculator 2026 | CO Home Loan Payment Estimator
colorado.metaDescription: Calculate your Colorado mortgage payment. CO median home price ~$540K, very low property taxes (0.51%), wildfire insurance considerations. Market has cooled from 2022 peak. Free CO calculator 2026.
colorado.intro: Colorado's housing market boomed dramatically during the pandemic era before cooling from its 2022 peak, offering buyers more options and negotiating power. Colorado's very low property tax rate (0.51%) partially offsets the high purchase prices, though wildfire insurance costs have become a significant factor in many areas.
colorado.taxContent: Colorado's 0.51% property tax rate is one of the lowest in the nation. On a $540,000 home, expect ~$2,750/year. The state income tax is a flat 4.4%. Colorado also offers a Senior Homestead Exemption for qualifying seniors.
colorado.insuranceContent: The 2021 Marshall Fire and other recent fires have caused some insurers to exit Colorado or dramatically increase premiums in high-risk areas. Mountain and foothills properties face the highest costs. Hail is also a major risk on the Front Range. Budget $1,500–$4,000+/year.
colorado.marketContent: Denver metro prices have moderated from their 2022 peak with more inventory. Boulder remains expensive. Colorado Springs has grown driven by military installations. Mountain resort communities (Aspen, Vail) have ultra-premium pricing. Fort Collins offers more affordable Front Range options.

connecticut.name: Connecticut

statePrograms.name: CHFA First-Time Homebuyer Program
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rate mortgages for first-time homebuyers within CHFA income limits.

connecticut.metaTitle: Connecticut Mortgage Calculator 2026 | CT Home Loan Payment Estimator
connecticut.metaDescription: Calculate your Connecticut mortgage payment. CT has very high property taxes (avg 2.14%) — a critical factor in your total monthly payment. Free CT calculator for 2026.
connecticut.intro: Connecticut's housing market has been revived by NYC residents seeking more space while maintaining Metro-North commuting access. However, property taxes averaging 2.14% — among the highest in the nation — add $600–$1,000+ per month to housing costs that many buyers underestimate.
connecticut.taxContent: Connecticut's 2.14% average property tax rate means a $380,000 home costs ~$678/month in taxes alone. Rates vary significantly by municipality. The state income tax tops out at 6.99%. This combined tax burden is a primary consideration for buyers.
connecticut.insuranceContent: Insurance costs are moderate at $1,200–$1,600/year for most properties. Coastal Fairfield and New Haven counties face some hurricane and nor'easter risk. Older housing stock (many homes from the 1800s) can affect premiums. Budget $100–$135/month.
connecticut.marketContent: Fairfield County (Greenwich, Westport, Darien) has seen strong demand from NYC buyers seeking larger homes with Metro-North access. Hartford and New Haven offer more affordable options. Connecticut's tight inventory supports prices despite the high tax burden.

delaware.name: Delaware

statePrograms.name: DSHA First-Time Homebuyer Program
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rates and down payment assistance for first-time homebuyers in Delaware.

delaware.metaTitle: Delaware Mortgage Calculator 2026 | DE Home Loan Payment Estimator
delaware.metaDescription: Calculate your Delaware mortgage payment. Delaware has no sales tax and low property taxes (0.57%). Popular with Philadelphia-area buyers. Free DE calculator for 2026.
delaware.intro: Delaware offers a compelling combination of low property taxes (0.57%), no state sales tax, and proximity to Philadelphia, Baltimore, and Washington D.C. The state has become increasingly popular with buyers priced out of Pennsylvania and New Jersey.
delaware.taxContent: Delaware's 0.57% property tax rate is well below the national average — on a $330,000 home, about $157/month. There is no state sales tax. Income tax ranges from 2.2% to 6.6%. The Senior School Property Tax Credit provides additional relief for qualifying seniors.
delaware.insuranceContent: Delaware Beach communities (Rehoboth, Dewey, Bethany) face flood and hurricane risk requiring NFIP flood insurance ($700–$1,500/year). Inland areas average $900–$1,300/year. Budget $75–$110/month.
delaware.marketContent: New Castle County (Wilmington, Newark, Middletown) benefits from proximity to Philadelphia and has attracted significant corporate presence. Kent and Sussex counties offer more affordable options and strong retiree demand. Delaware's small size makes cross-county commuting practical.

florida.name: Florida

statePrograms.name: Florida Assist (FL Assist)
statePrograms.description: Up to $10,000 in down payment assistance as a 0% interest deferred second mortgage for qualifying buyers.

florida.metaTitle: Florida Mortgage Calculator 2026 | FL Home Loan Payment Estimator
florida.metaDescription: Calculate your Florida mortgage payment. FL has no income tax but high insurance costs ($200–$500/mo in coastal areas). Homestead Exemption available. Free FL calculator 2026.
florida.intro: Florida's no-income-tax advantage is real, but homeowners insurance costs — driven by a troubled insurance market and hurricane risk — can add $200–$500+ per month to your housing costs in coastal areas. This is the single most important factor Florida buyers often underestimate.
florida.taxContent: Florida has no state income tax. The Homestead Exemption exempts the first $50,000 of assessed value from property taxes for primary residents. The Save Our Homes cap limits annual assessment increases to 3% for homesteaded properties, protecting long-term owners.
florida.insuranceContent: Florida's insurance market is in crisis — multiple major insurers have exited the state. Coastal annual premiums range from $4,000 to $15,000+. Windstorm insurance is often required separately in coastal counties. Flood insurance is mandatory in FEMA flood zones. Budget $200–$500+/month in coastal areas, $150–$250 inland.
florida.marketContent: Florida boomed during 2020–2022 and has since cooled with rising inventory. Miami has strong international demand. Tampa and Orlando saw the most significant corrections from peak. Jacksonville is the most affordable major metro. New construction is abundant in many markets.

georgia.name: Georgia

statePrograms.name: Georgia Dream Homeownership Program
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rate mortgages with down payment assistance of $7,500–$10,000 for qualifying first-time buyers.

georgia.metaTitle: Georgia Mortgage Calculator 2026 | GA Home Loan Payment Estimator
georgia.metaDescription: Calculate your Georgia mortgage payment with current GA rates and property taxes (avg 0.92%). Atlanta is one of the Southeast's most dynamic markets. Free GA calculator 2026.
georgia.intro: Georgia's housing market is anchored by Atlanta, one of the fastest-growing and most economically dynamic regions in the Southeast, attracting major corporate relocations including Apple, Microsoft, and numerous Fortune 500 companies. Outside Atlanta, Savannah and Augusta offer distinct market opportunities.
georgia.taxContent: Georgia's 0.92% property tax rate is close to the national average. The Homestead Exemption reduces assessed value for primary residences. Georgia's income tax is being phased to a flat 5.39%, making it increasingly competitive.
georgia.insuranceContent: Coastal areas (Savannah, Brunswick) face hurricane and flood risk with higher premiums. Atlanta and inland areas average $1,400–$2,000/year. Severe thunderstorms and occasional tornadoes affect the state. Budget $120–$180/month.
georgia.marketContent: Atlanta has been one of the most active Southeast markets, driven by tech, film, logistics, and healthcare. Intown neighborhoods command premium prices while suburbs like Alpharetta and Marietta offer more space. Savannah has seen strong appreciation. Much of rural Georgia qualifies for USDA loans.

hawaii.name: Hawaii

statePrograms.name: HHFDC Mortgage Credit Certificate
statePrograms.agency: Hawaii Housing Finance and Development Corporation
statePrograms.description: Federal tax credit of up to 20% of annual mortgage interest paid, reducing federal income tax liability for qualifying first-time buyers.

hawaii.metaTitle: Hawaii Mortgage Calculator 2026 | HI Home Loan Payment Estimator
hawaii.metaDescription: Calculate your Hawaii mortgage payment. Hawaii median home price ~$830K, lowest property tax rate in the nation (0.28%), high conforming loan limits ($1,149,825). Free HI calculator 2026.
hawaii.intro: Hawaii has the highest home prices of any state and the lowest property tax rate in the nation — a paradox reflecting island economics. The median home price on Oahu exceeds $800,000, and Maui has seen dramatic appreciation driven by second-home demand. Hawaii's high conforming loan limit ($1,149,825) helps many buyers avoid jumbo loans.
hawaii.taxContent: Hawaii's 0.28% property tax rate is the lowest in the U.S. — on an $830,000 home, just ~$193/month. The Home Exemption reduces assessed value by $100,000 for owner-occupants. However, Hawaii has the highest state income tax in the nation (up to 11%).
hawaii.insuranceContent: Earthquake and volcanic activity risk is significant, especially on the Big Island where lava flows have destroyed neighborhoods. Standard insurance doesn't cover volcanic activity or earthquakes. Hurricane risk is real but less frequent than Gulf Coast states. Budget $900–$1,500/year for standard coverage plus earthquake insurance.
hawaii.marketContent: Oahu is the most active market with a range from urban Honolulu condos to suburban Kailua homes. Maui has seen dramatic appreciation with medians exceeding $1M. The Big Island offers the most affordable options within the state. Hawaii's $1,149,825 conforming limit is essential for most buyers.

idaho.name: Idaho

statePrograms.name: Idaho Housing First Loan
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rate mortgages with optional down payment assistance for qualifying Idaho homebuyers.

idaho.metaTitle: Idaho Mortgage Calculator 2026 | ID Home Loan Payment Estimator
idaho.metaDescription: Calculate your Idaho mortgage payment. Idaho median home price ~$430K, low property taxes (0.69%), community property state. Boise market has cooled from 2022 peak. Free ID calculator 2026.
idaho.intro: Idaho — particularly the Boise metro — experienced one of the most dramatic housing booms in the nation during 2020–2022 as remote workers from California and the Pacific Northwest flooded in. After prices peaked, the market has cooled significantly, offering buyers more options. Idaho is a community property state.
idaho.taxContent: Idaho's 0.69% property tax rate is below the national average. The Homeowner's Exemption exempts 50% of a home's value (up to $125,000) from property taxes — effectively cutting your tax bill in half on many homes. Idaho is a community property state with a flat 5.8% income tax.
idaho.insuranceContent: Wildfire risk is significant in forested northern Idaho (Coeur d'Alene, Sandpoint) and Boise foothills. Insurance costs have increased substantially in high-risk areas. Boise metro averages $900–$1,400/year. Budget $80–$150/month depending on location.
idaho.marketContent: The Treasure Valley (Boise, Nampa, Meridian) saw extraordinary growth during the pandemic era before correcting 10–15% from peak. The market has stabilized. Coeur d'Alene has seen strong demand from Pacific Northwest buyers. Much of rural Idaho qualifies for USDA loans.

illinois.name: Illinois

statePrograms.name: IHDA Access Forgivable
statePrograms.description: 4% of the purchase price (up to $6,000) in forgivable down payment assistance for qualifying first-time buyers.

illinois.metaTitle: Illinois Mortgage Calculator 2026 | IL Home Loan Payment Estimator
illinois.metaDescription: Calculate your Illinois mortgage payment. IL has the 2nd highest property taxes in the nation (avg 2.27%) — a critical factor in your monthly payment. Free IL calculator 2026.
illinois.intro: Illinois has the second-highest property tax rate in the nation at 2.27%, which is the single most important factor for buyers to understand. On a $270,000 home, property taxes alone add approximately $511 per month to your payment — more than many buyers expect.
illinois.taxContent: Illinois's 2.27% average property tax rate means a $270,000 home costs ~$511/month in taxes. Rates vary significantly by county and municipality. The General Homestead Exemption reduces assessed value by $10,000. The state income tax is a flat 4.95%.
illinois.insuranceContent: Illinois has moderate insurance costs at $1,200–$1,600/year. Tornado risk affects central and southern Illinois. Flooding is a concern near the Chicago River and in low-lying communities. Budget $100–$140/month.
illinois.marketContent: Chicago offers a wide range of prices from affordable South and West Side neighborhoods to premium Lincoln Park and North Shore suburbs. The market has been relatively stable with modest appreciation. Downstate Illinois (Peoria, Springfield, Rockford) offers very affordable housing. High property taxes are a primary driver of population outmigration.

indiana.name: Indiana

statePrograms.name: IHCDA Next Home Program
statePrograms.agency: Indiana Housing and Community Development Authority
statePrograms.description: Down payment assistance of 3.5% of the purchase price for qualifying homebuyers in Indiana.

indiana.metaTitle: Indiana Mortgage Calculator 2026 | IN Home Loan Payment Estimator
indiana.metaDescription: Calculate your Indiana mortgage payment. Indiana median home price ~$240K with low property taxes (0.85%). Indianapolis is one of the Midwest's most affordable major metros. Free IN calculator 2026.
indiana.intro: Indiana offers some of the most affordable homeownership in the Midwest, with a median home price around $240,000 and a flat income tax rate of just 3.05% — one of the lowest in the nation. Indianapolis has emerged as a major Midwest hub for tech, life sciences, and logistics.
indiana.taxContent: Indiana's 0.85% property tax rate is close to the national average. The Homestead Standard Deduction reduces assessed value by 60% (up to $45,000) for primary residences — a significant benefit. Indiana's flat 3.05% income tax is one of the lowest flat rates in the nation.
indiana.insuranceContent: Indiana faces tornado risk, particularly in central and southern parts of the state. Annual insurance averages $1,100–$1,500. Flooding is a concern along the Ohio River in southern Indiana. Budget $90–$130/month.
indiana.marketContent: Indianapolis has seen steady appreciation driven by tech, life sciences (Eli Lilly, Salesforce), and logistics. The metro offers excellent value compared to Chicago, Columbus, or Cincinnati. Carmel and Fishers are among the fastest-growing Midwest communities. Much of rural Indiana qualifies for USDA loans.

iowa.name: Iowa

statePrograms.name: IFA FirstHome Program
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rate mortgages with optional down payment assistance for first-time homebuyers in Iowa.

iowa.metaTitle: Iowa Mortgage Calculator 2026 | IA Home Loan Payment Estimator
iowa.metaDescription: Calculate your Iowa mortgage payment. Iowa median home price ~$210K but property taxes are above average (1.57%). Large USDA-eligible rural areas. Free IA calculator 2026.
iowa.intro: Iowa offers very affordable home prices — among the lowest in the Midwest — but buyers should be aware that property taxes are above the national average at 1.57%. Des Moines has emerged as a major insurance and financial services hub, providing stable, high-quality employment.
iowa.taxContent: Iowa's 1.57% property tax rate is above the national average — on a $210,000 home, about $275/month. Iowa offers a Homestead Tax Credit for primary residences. The state income tax is being reformed toward a flat 3.9% rate by 2026, down from rates as high as 8.53%.
iowa.insuranceContent: Iowa sits in the heart of Tornado Alley. The 2020 derecho caused billions in damage across the state. Annual insurance averages $1,300–$1,800. Flooding is a concern along the Missouri and Mississippi rivers. Budget $110–$150/month.
iowa.marketContent: Des Moines consistently ranks among the most affordable major metros in the nation while offering strong job opportunities in insurance, financial services, and tech. Cedar Rapids and the Quad Cities offer additional affordable options. Iowa has extensive USDA-eligible rural areas.

kansas.name: Kansas

statePrograms.name: KHRC First Time Homebuyer Program
statePrograms.description: Down payment assistance and below-market interest rates for qualifying first-time homebuyers in Kansas.

kansas.metaTitle: Kansas Mortgage Calculator 2026 | KS Home Loan Payment Estimator
kansas.metaDescription: Calculate your Kansas mortgage payment. Kansas median home price ~$215K but high insurance costs due to tornado risk ($170–$250/mo). Property taxes average 1.41%. Free KS calculator 2026.
kansas.intro: Kansas offers some of the most affordable home prices in the nation, but buyers must factor in above-average property taxes (1.41%) and some of the highest homeowners insurance costs in the country due to the state's location in Tornado Alley.
kansas.taxContent: Kansas's 1.41% property tax rate is above the national average. On a $215,000 home, about $252/month in taxes. Kansas offers a Homestead Refund for qualifying low-income homeowners. The state income tax tops out at 5.7%.
kansas.insuranceContent: Kansas is in the heart of Tornado Alley — annual premiums average $2,000–$3,000, with some areas seeing $3,500–$5,000. Hail damage is extremely common. Budget $170–$250+/month for homeowners insurance — this is a critical cost to include in your monthly payment calculation.
kansas.marketContent: Kansas City's Johnson County suburbs (Overland Park, Olathe) offer excellent schools and strong job market access at prices below the Missouri side of KC. Wichita is the state's largest standalone city, with a stable aviation manufacturing economy. Much of rural Kansas qualifies for USDA loans.

kentucky.name: Kentucky

statePrograms.name: KHC Regular Down Payment Assistance
statePrograms.description: Down payment assistance of $7,500 as a 10-year deferred loan for qualifying first-time homebuyers.

kentucky.metaTitle: Kentucky Mortgage Calculator 2026 | KY Home Loan Payment Estimator
kentucky.metaDescription: Calculate your Kentucky mortgage payment. Kentucky median home price ~$210K with low property taxes (0.86%). One of the most affordable states for homebuyers. Free KY calculator 2026.
kentucky.intro: Kentucky consistently ranks among the most affordable states for homeownership, with a median home price around $210,000 and a flat income tax rate of 4.5%. Louisville and Lexington offer urban amenities at prices that would be unimaginable in coastal cities.
kentucky.taxContent: Kentucky's 0.86% property tax rate is close to the national average. The Homestead Exemption reduces assessed value by $46,350 for primary residences. Kentucky's flat 4.5% income tax is moderate. The overall tax burden is quite low, contributing to affordability.
kentucky.insuranceContent: Kentucky faces tornado risk, particularly in the western part of the state. The December 2021 tornado outbreak caused catastrophic damage in western Kentucky. Annual insurance averages $1,300–$1,800. Budget $110–$150/month.
kentucky.marketContent: Louisville has a diverse economy spanning healthcare, logistics (UPS Worldport), and bourbon manufacturing. Lexington is home to the University of Kentucky and a significant horse industry. Both cities offer strong value compared to peer cities. Fort Knox and Fort Campbell support military housing demand. Much of rural Kentucky qualifies for USDA loans.

louisiana.name: Louisiana

statePrograms.name: LHC Soft Second Program
statePrograms.description: Soft second mortgage providing down payment and closing cost assistance for qualifying first-time homebuyers.

louisiana.metaTitle: Louisiana Mortgage Calculator 2026 | LA Home Loan Payment Estimator
louisiana.metaDescription: Calculate your Louisiana mortgage payment. LA has low property taxes (0.55%) but very high insurance costs due to hurricane risk ($200–$400/mo). Community property state. Free LA calculator 2026.
louisiana.intro: Louisiana presents a unique financial picture: very low property taxes (0.55%) but some of the highest homeowners insurance costs in the nation due to hurricane and flood risk. The insurance crisis in Louisiana has become severe, with multiple major insurers exiting the state. Budget carefully — insurance can easily exceed $200–$400/month in coastal and flood-prone areas.
louisiana.taxContent: Louisiana's 0.55% property tax rate is one of the lowest in the nation. The Homestead Exemption exempts the first $75,000 of assessed value from property taxes for primary residents — eliminating taxes entirely for many lower-value homes. Louisiana is a community property state with a 4.25% top income tax rate.
louisiana.insuranceContent: Louisiana's insurance market is in crisis following Hurricanes Katrina, Ida, and numerous other storms. Coastal annual premiums range from $4,000 to $15,000+. Flood insurance is mandatory in FEMA flood zones and strongly recommended elsewhere. Budget $200–$400+/month in coastal areas, $150–$250 inland.
louisiana.marketContent: New Orleans offers unique cultural heritage with a range of prices from affordable neighborhoods to premium Garden District properties. Baton Rouge is the state capital with a stable market. Shreveport and Lafayette offer affordable options. Louisiana is a community property state affecting how jointly-owned property is treated.

maine.name: Maine

statePrograms.name: MaineHousing First Home Loan Program
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rate mortgages with optional Advantage down payment assistance for qualifying first-time buyers.

maine.metaTitle: Maine Mortgage Calculator 2026 | ME Home Loan Payment Estimator
maine.metaDescription: Calculate your Maine mortgage payment. Maine median home price ~$360K, property taxes avg 1.36%. Portland market has seen strong appreciation from remote work demand. Free ME calculator 2026.
maine.intro: Maine's housing market has been transformed by the remote work era, with Portland emerging as one of the most desirable small cities in the Northeast. The state's natural beauty and relatively affordable prices compared to Boston have attracted significant in-migration, though inventory is extremely tight.
maine.taxContent: Maine's 1.36% property tax rate is above the national average. The Homestead Exemption reduces assessed value by $25,000 for primary residences. Maine's income tax tops out at 7.15%. The Property Tax Fairness Credit provides some relief for lower-income homeowners.
maine.insuranceContent: Maine's insurance costs are moderate at $900–$1,300/year. The state faces winter storm risk including heavy snow, ice storms, and nor'easters. Coastal properties face some flood and storm surge risk. Budget $75–$110/month.
maine.marketContent: Portland has seen dramatic appreciation driven by remote workers and lifestyle buyers from Boston and New York. The rest of the state offers more affordable options, though inventory is tight statewide. Maine has significant USDA-eligible rural areas. The short construction season and limited buildable land constrain new supply.

maryland.name: Maryland

statePrograms.name: Maryland Mortgage Program
statePrograms.agency: Maryland Department of Housing and Community Development
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rate mortgages with down payment assistance of up to $5,000 for qualifying first-time buyers.

maryland.metaTitle: Maryland Mortgage Calculator 2026 | MD Home Loan Payment Estimator
maryland.metaDescription: Calculate your Maryland mortgage payment. MD has high conforming loan limits ($1,149,825) and a strong federal employment base. Property taxes avg 1.09%. Free MD calculator 2026.
maryland.intro: Maryland's housing market is heavily influenced by its proximity to Washington D.C. and the massive federal government employment base. Montgomery and Prince George's counties have some of the highest median incomes and home prices in the state. Maryland has high conforming loan limits ($1,149,825) in many counties.
maryland.taxContent: Maryland's 1.09% property tax rate is close to the national average. The Homestead Tax Credit caps annual assessment increases at 10% for primary residences. Maryland has a progressive income tax up to 5.75% plus county income taxes of 2.25–3.2%, creating a significant combined burden.
maryland.insuranceContent: Insurance costs are moderate at $1,000–$1,400/year. Coastal areas (Eastern Shore, Chesapeake Bay) face flood and storm surge risk. Flood insurance is required in many FEMA flood zones. Budget $85–$120/month.
maryland.marketContent: The DC suburbs (Montgomery, Prince George's, Howard, Anne Arundel counties) have some of the highest prices and strongest demand in the Mid-Atlantic, driven by federal employment and defense contractors. Baltimore offers more affordable options with ongoing revitalization. Maryland's $1,149,825 conforming limit is a significant advantage for DC suburb buyers.

massachusetts.name: Massachusetts

statePrograms.name: MassHousing Down Payment Assistance
statePrograms.description: Down payment assistance of up to 5% of the purchase price (up to $15,000) for qualifying first-time buyers.

massachusetts.metaTitle: Massachusetts Mortgage Calculator 2026 | MA Home Loan Payment Estimator
massachusetts.metaDescription: Calculate your Massachusetts mortgage payment. MA median home price ~$580K, high conforming loan limits ($1,149,825). Boston is one of the nation's most expensive markets. Free MA calculator 2026.
massachusetts.intro: Massachusetts has one of the most expensive housing markets in the nation, driven by Boston's world-class universities, biotech and life sciences industry, and financial sector. The Greater Boston area has a median home price well above $700,000, with limited inventory and intense competition.
massachusetts.taxContent: Massachusetts's 1.23% property tax rate is close to the national average. Many municipalities offer a Residential Exemption for owner-occupants. The state income tax is a flat 5% (with a 4% surtax on income over $1M). A real estate transfer tax of $4.56 per $1,000 adds to closing costs.
massachusetts.insuranceContent: Insurance costs are moderate at $1,200–$1,600/year. The state faces nor'easter and winter storm risk. Coastal properties face flood and storm surge risk. Cape Cod and the Islands have higher costs due to coastal exposure. Budget $100–$135/month.
massachusetts.marketContent: Greater Boston is one of the most expensive and competitive markets in the nation, with homes often selling above asking price within days. Cambridge, Somerville, and Brookline command premium prices. Worcester and Springfield offer more affordable alternatives. Massachusetts's $1,149,825 conforming limit is essential for Boston metro buyers.

michigan.name: Michigan

statePrograms.name: MSHDA MI Home Loan
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rate mortgages with down payment assistance of up to $10,000 for qualifying first-time buyers.

michigan.metaTitle: Michigan Mortgage Calculator 2026 | MI Home Loan Payment Estimator
michigan.metaDescription: Calculate your Michigan mortgage payment. Michigan median home price ~$240K but above-average property taxes (1.54%). Detroit and Grand Rapids are growing markets. Free MI calculator 2026.
michigan.intro: Michigan offers affordable home prices but above-average property taxes (1.54%), creating a nuanced affordability picture. Detroit is undergoing significant revitalization and Grand Rapids has emerged as one of the Midwest's most dynamic mid-size cities.
michigan.taxContent: Michigan's 1.54% property tax rate is above the national average. The Principal Residence Exemption (PRE) reduces taxable value for primary residences by 18 mills (1.8%), providing significant savings. Michigan's income tax is a flat 4.25%.
michigan.insuranceContent: Michigan's insurance costs are moderate at $1,100–$1,500/year. The state faces winter storm risk including heavy lake-effect snow in western Michigan. Flooding is a concern in southeastern Michigan. Budget $90–$130/month.
michigan.marketContent: Detroit has undergone significant revitalization with neighborhoods like Midtown, Corktown, and New Center attracting investment. Ann Arbor (University of Michigan) and Troy offer strong schools and amenities. Grand Rapids has a dynamic economy driven by healthcare, manufacturing, and tech. The Great Lakes shoreline creates premium vacation markets in Traverse City and Petoskey.

minnesota.name: Minnesota

statePrograms.name: Minnesota Housing Start Up Program
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rate mortgages with optional down payment assistance for first-time homebuyers in Minnesota.

minnesota.metaTitle: Minnesota Mortgage Calculator 2026 | MN Home Loan Payment Estimator
minnesota.metaDescription: Calculate your Minnesota mortgage payment. Minnesota median home price ~$330K, moderate property taxes (1.12%). Minneapolis-St. Paul is home to 19 Fortune 500 companies. Free MN calculator 2026.
minnesota.intro: Minnesota's Twin Cities metro is one of the most economically diverse and stable major metros in the Midwest, home to 19 Fortune 500 companies including Target, UnitedHealth Group, and 3M. The state's housing market has been relatively stable with steady appreciation and strong fundamentals.
minnesota.taxContent: Minnesota's 1.12% property tax rate is close to the national average. The Homestead Market Value Exclusion reduces taxable market value for primary residences. Minnesota's income tax tops out at 9.85% — one of the highest in the nation. The Property Tax Refund program provides relief for homeowners with high taxes relative to income.
minnesota.insuranceContent: Minnesota's insurance costs are moderate at $1,300–$1,700/year. The state faces significant winter storm risk including heavy snowfall, ice storms, and extreme cold. Severe thunderstorms and occasional tornadoes affect the southern part of the state. Budget $110–$145/month.
minnesota.marketContent: The Twin Cities metro offers a wide range of prices from affordable Minneapolis and St. Paul neighborhoods to premium suburbs like Edina, Minnetonka, and Wayzata. Rochester (Mayo Clinic) has a strong, stable market. Duluth has seen increased interest from remote workers. Much of greater Minnesota qualifies for USDA loans.

mississippi.name: Mississippi

statePrograms.name: MHC Smart6 Program
statePrograms.description: Down payment assistance of up to 6% of the loan amount for qualifying first-time homebuyers in Mississippi.

mississippi.metaTitle: Mississippi Mortgage Calculator 2026 | MS Home Loan Payment Estimator
mississippi.metaDescription: Calculate your Mississippi mortgage payment. Mississippi has the lowest median home prices in the nation (~$175K) with low property taxes (0.65%). Free MS calculator 2026.
mississippi.intro: Mississippi has the lowest median home prices of any state in the nation, making it one of the most accessible states for first-time homebuyers on a budget. The state's low property taxes (0.65%) and low cost of living further enhance affordability, though hurricane risk along the Gulf Coast drives higher insurance costs.
mississippi.taxContent: Mississippi's 0.65% property tax rate is well below the national average. The Homestead Exemption exempts the first $7,500 of assessed value from all ad valorem taxes for primary residences. Mississippi's income tax is a flat 5% being phased down to 4% by 2026.
mississippi.insuranceContent: The Gulf Coast (Gulfport, Biloxi, Pascagoula) faces significant hurricane risk with annual premiums of $3,000–$8,000+. Inland areas average $1,500–$2,200/year. The state also faces tornado risk and severe thunderstorms. Budget $125–$200/month depending on location.
mississippi.marketContent: Mississippi's housing market is among the most affordable in the nation. The Gulf Coast has a more dynamic market driven by gaming, tourism, and military (Keesler AFB). Southaven (Memphis suburb) has seen strong growth. Much of rural Mississippi qualifies for USDA loans, making zero-down homeownership widely accessible.

missouri.name: Missouri

statePrograms.name: MHDC First Place Loan
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rate mortgages with optional cash assistance for down payment and closing costs for qualifying first-time buyers.

missouri.metaTitle: Missouri Mortgage Calculator 2026 | MO Home Loan Payment Estimator
missouri.metaDescription: Calculate your Missouri mortgage payment. Missouri median home price ~$230K with moderate property taxes (1.01%). Kansas City and St. Louis are major economic hubs. Free MO calculator 2026.
missouri.intro: Missouri offers affordable homeownership with a median price around $230,000 and moderate property taxes. The state has two major metros — Kansas City and St. Louis — each with distinct economic profiles. Kansas City has emerged as a tech and startup hub, while St. Louis has a strong healthcare and financial services sector.
missouri.taxContent: Missouri's 1.01% property tax rate is close to the national average. The state offers a Senior Citizens Property Tax Credit for qualifying seniors. Missouri's income tax tops out at 4.95% (being reduced over time). Property taxes vary significantly by county.
missouri.insuranceContent: Missouri sits in a severe weather corridor with tornado risk, severe thunderstorms, and occasional flooding. The New Madrid Seismic Zone in southeastern Missouri also presents earthquake risk. Annual insurance averages $1,500–$2,000. Budget $125–$170/month.
missouri.marketContent: Kansas City has seen strong growth driven by tech, healthcare, and logistics, with neighborhoods like the Crossroads Arts District attracting young professionals. St. Louis offers very affordable prices with revitalization in areas like the Central West End. Columbia (University of Missouri) has a stable university-driven market. Much of rural Missouri qualifies for USDA loans.
